summary: Profile layer solved sparse entries, but exit_signal remains structurally loss-making across multiple profile-only exit tuning attempts.
evidence: paper_baseline validation had 1 trade / 61 days; candidate_density_20260409_a raised validation to 21 trades and trades_per_day to 0.3443, so entry sparsity is no longer the primary blocker.
candidate_density_20260409_a validation still failed with profit_total=-0.003322 and profit_factor=0.8388; exit_signal contributed 11 exits with total -12.980 USDT.
candidate_exit_20260409_a improved validation to profit_total=-0.000986 and profit_factor=0.9543, but exit_signal still contributed 10 exits with total -19.485 USDT and only 10.0% winrate.
candidate_exit_20260409_a test remained negative at -2.10% with profit_factor=0.58; exit_signal contributed 11 exits with total -49.598 USDT and 0% winrate.

candidate_exit_20260409_b and candidate_exit_20260409_c changed exit-side thresholds, stoploss, ROI, and trailing behavior but validation remained below gate, confirming parameter-only tuning is saturating.
attempted_profile_changes: reduced ma period from 276 to 220 and shortened rsi period from 15 to 12
relaxed rsi entry/exit thresholds from 26.6/78.6 to 31.5/73.0
tightened stoploss from -0.251 to -0.08 and then -0.05
compressed minimal_roi ladder from 0.253/0.118/0.043/0 to 0.018/0.01/0.004/0 and then 0.012/0.008/0.003/0
reduced trailing_stop_positive and trailing_stop_positive_offset to make trailing exits trigger earlier
tested more aggressive rsi thresholds at 33.5/69.0 and observed validation regression
why_profile_layer_failed: Profile-level knobs can change threshold placement and risk parameters, but they cannot redesign the exit condition structure itself. After signal density was restored, losses remained concentrated in exit_signal rather than in sparse-entry failure. Multiple profile-only exit tuning attempts improved validation toward breakeven but could not produce a stable profit_factor above 1, and test performance remained clearly negative.
requested_spec_changes: review whether the current exit condition '(rsi extreme) OR (price crossing ma)' is too coarse and exits too late after reversal confirmation
consider asymmetric long/short exit logic instead of reusing the same threshold family for both entry and exit
consider adding derived indicators or exit guards that detect momentum loss earlier than the current ma cross
review whether exit logic should separate profit-taking from reversal-stop behavior instead of relying on the same boolean expression
expected_outcome: Reduce structural losses attributed to exit_signal while keeping the improved sample density achieved at the profile layer.
